tldr: The 2026 Giro is in progress — the race began 8 May in Bulgaria, finishes 31 May in Milan. This is a hub page for the stages we've published so far; the full race overview (GC, classifications, full team narratives, aftermath) will be written after the final stage rolls into Milan. Stages 1-7 are individually covered with podiums, narratives, and per-tracked-rider performances. Stage 8 onwards land each day as the race rolls.

narrative.headline: Race in progress — stages published as they finish
narrative.openingMoves: Bulgarian Grande Partenza: Magnier won the opening sprint into Burgas (Stage 1), Silva took the small-group sprint into Veliko Tarnovo (Stage 2) on a day defined by a descent crash that wiped out four UAE Team Emirates riders simultaneously (Vine, Soler, Adam Yates, Baroncini all withdrawn), Magnier doubled up with a second sprint win in Sofia (Stage 3).
narrative.raceUnfolds: After the rest-day transfer to Italy: Narváez salvaged UAE's first stage win in Cosenza (Stage 4), Igor Arrieta won a rain-affected hilly day to Potenza (Stage 5), Ballerini took a cobbled sprint into Naples (Stage 6).
narrative.decision: Stage 7 — the Blockhaus 244 km queen stage — went to Vingegaard, 13 seconds clear of Gall, with Pellizzari 4th at +1:05 making the GC top-five on a home Italian mountain. The Giro's GC question is now mostly Visma's race to lose, with Decathlon's Gall the primary credible challenger.
narrative.finale: Stages 8 onwards will be added as they finish. Final overall GC will be written once the race is over.

decisiveMoments.headline: UAE's Bulgarian disaster
decisiveMoments.description: Mass crash on the rain-soaked descent into Veliko Tarnovo, ~21-23 km to go. Four UAE riders down at once. Vine (broken elbow + concussion), Soler (fractured pelvis), Adam Yates (delayed concussion), Baroncini all out within hours.

decisiveMoments.headline: Vingegaard's GC claim
decisiveMoments.description: 13.6 km final climb, 244 km stage, the longest of the Giro. Vingegaard attacked, only Gall could hold to within 13 seconds, the rest of the GC field lost a minute or more. Pellizzari held 4th.

aftermath.headline: Mid-race summary
aftermath.body: The Giro's first week was defined by Bulgarian sprinting (Magnier × 2, Ballerini), UAE's Bulgarian descent disaster (four riders out from a single crash), Italian climbing rebellion (Arrieta the surprise of Stage 5, Pellizzari emerging across multiple stages), and then Vingegaard claiming the GC with a Blockhaus win that confirmed the Paris-Nice form line. The final two weeks will decide whether Gall can press the gap closer, whether Pellizzari can climb onto the podium, and whether anyone else still has a card to play.
